title: World IP Day 2026: Uneven Performance or Team Europe?
content:
Download KR21’s infographic

A key justification for intellectual property rules is their contribution to innovation. The prevailing logic suggests that the creation of (time-limited) monopolies allows creators to recoup the costs of developing new ideas.

This argument is relatively straightforward for measures such as patents, trademarks, or design rights (although concerns about abuses persist). 

However, it is more complicated regarding copyright. This doesn’t just regulate the creative industries, but equally affects citizens, scientists and researchers. The research process is explicitly about accessing and building on existing facts and insights. As such, anything that causes barriers to, or exclusion from, this needs to be strictly evaluated. Copyright needs to be designed with this objective in mind. 

Picking up (tangentially) on the theme of this year’s World Intellectual Property Day – IP and sport – we’re lo
